rules: [P1] For requested measurements, search all relevant observation sources and measurement labels/codes before concluding no data exist.

[P1] For order-management tasks, first search broadly for qualifying active orders across relevant order types, statuses, aliases, and care settings; match orders to the requested clinical intent rather than drug/class alone; verify intent-specific regimen details from dose, frequency, and order context; only create a new order if no equivalent active order exists; if exactly one appropriate active order exists, keep it; reconcile duplicates only among clinically equivalent orders by keeping the newest appropriate active order and discontinuing older ones.
[P1] If the protocol's trigger condition is not met, take no protocol-driven action.

[P1] Before creating orders in workflows that may require reconciliation, verify that the available tools support needed update/discontinue actions; if not, avoid creating an uncorrectable duplicate state and report the exact keep/discontinue reconciliation plan plus the tool limitation.
[P1] Escalate missing-data conclusions only after exhausting all relevant record sources, including active documentation and order/discontinuation records.
[P1] If a requested prerequisite, related, or companion result is absent after searching relevant sources, report it explicitly as not found.
[P1] Verify that treatment-start criteria require the full documented pattern, not a single qualifying value.
[P1] Do not recommend protocol-driven follow-up when the latest relevant value is within the protocol's normal or non-triggering range.
[P1] Use domain-specific codes, aliases, synonyms, and all relevant record sections to identify prior qualifying events or active treatments.

[P1] For imaging reorders, search imaging history broadly by study name/synonyms across results/history, use only completed qualifying diagnostic studies (excluding reference-only entries unless the protocol allows them), match modality/body region/variant exactly, then calculate recency explicitly from the most recent qualifying study date to today before deciding whether to reorder.
[P1] Verify time-threshold calculations explicitly against the current date instead of relying on rough recency judgments.
[P1] When companion data are missing, apply the protocol to the available qualifying result and note the limitation.
[P1] Start with patient-scoped queries against the specific relevant resource(s), labels/codes, and time filters when applicable before broader or paginated record traversal.
[P1] If required protocol inputs are absent, do not infer or execute protocol-driven treatment or repeat-order actions; state clearly that protocol execution is blocked by missing qualifying data.

[P1] For protocol-driven companion or safety orders, first identify the qualifying active trigger treatment set (including class/alias coverage); if no qualifying trigger treatment is active, take no ordering action and report that none was found; otherwise add the companion only if no equivalent active companion order exists.

[P1] For repeat-order or interval-based decisions, identify the most recent completed qualifying study/procedure from results/history (not just orders), exclude scheduled or future-dated entries, verify the date is on or before today, and use that date.

[P1] For immunization recency and repeat-vaccine decisions, search immunization history and administration records (not just orders), match by vaccine family including equivalent codes/formulations, use the most recent documented administration date, and only order if no recent qualifying administration exists.
[P1] If no prior qualifying event is documented, treat the patient as due or overdue under the applicable time-based protocol.
[P1] Retrieve the most recent date and value for each requested lab or measurement independently; do not assume related tests were collected on the same date.
[P1] Do not place duration-based follow-up or removal orders when the current documented duration has not exceeded the protocol threshold.

[P1] For medication-based recommendations or safety checks, first establish the full patient-scoped active medication/order set without arbitrary date filtering, then check all relevant representations, aliases, and class-equivalent orders before concluding presence or absence.

[P1] Before applying duration-based management or placing follow-up/removal orders, confirm the target device or procedure through broad documentation sources (not just a single procedure code), determine duration from insertion or first documented presence across the full relevant history, and verify whether it is still active or has removal/discontinuation documentation.

[P1] For time-windowed averages or summaries, retrieve all qualifying timestamped observations from the correct measurement source, verify timestamps, filter separately for each requested window, and compute each window independently without reusing another interval's result.
[P1] Complete the requested workflow after retrieval: perform the needed calculations or decision step and report the requested result.
